# Codex

> Build with the Codex CLI, IDE extension, and cloud automation to speed up development.

Use this as a compact map of ChatGPT docs for Codex. Each page has a Markdown twin at `/codex/<slug>.md` for direct ingestion.

## Documentation sets
- [Combined ChatGPT docs](https://developers.openai.com/codex/llms-full.txt): Single-file Markdown export of ChatGPT and Codex guides and references.
- [Codex manual (Markdown)](https://developers.openai.com/codex/codex-manual.md): Machine-oriented condensed manual generated from the ChatGPT and Codex docs set.

## Administration
- [Administration](https://developers.openai.com/codex/administration.md): Set access and policy boundaries for ChatGPT, Codex developer tools, APIs, plugins, and connected systems

## Agent Approvals Security
- [Agent approvals & security](https://developers.openai.com/codex/agent-approvals-security.md): How to securely operate Codex with sandboxing, approvals, and network controls

## Agent Configuration
- [Custom instructions with AGENTS.md](https://developers.openai.com/codex/agent-configuration/agents-md.md): Give Codex extra instructions and context for your project
- [Rules](https://developers.openai.com/codex/agent-configuration/rules.md): Control which commands Codex can run outside the sandbox
- [Speed](https://developers.openai.com/codex/agent-configuration/speed.md): Increase speed without sacrificing intelligence
- [Subagents](https://developers.openai.com/codex/agent-configuration/subagents.md): Use subagents in ChatGPT and Codex, and configure custom Codex agents

## Amazon Bedrock
- [Use ChatGPT Work and Codex with Amazon Bedrock](https://developers.openai.com/codex/amazon-bedrock.md): Configure local ChatGPT Work and Codex surfaces to use OpenAI models available through Amazon Bedrock.

## App
- [ChatGPT desktop app](https://developers.openai.com/codex/app.md): Use the ChatGPT desktop app for projects, files, and long-running work.
- [ChatGPT desktop app commands](https://developers.openai.com/codex/app/commands.md): Reference for commands and keyboard shortcuts
- [ChatGPT desktop app for Windows](https://developers.openai.com/codex/app/windows.md): Use ChatGPT on Windows with native sandbox and PowerShell support
- [ChatGPT desktop app settings](https://developers.openai.com/codex/app/settings.md): Configure app behavior and preferences

## App Server
- [Codex App Server](https://developers.openai.com/codex/app-server.md): Embed Codex into your product with the app-server protocol

## Appshots
- [Appshots](https://developers.openai.com/codex/appshots.md): Give ChatGPT context from any Mac app

## Artifacts Viewer
- [Work with files](https://developers.openai.com/codex/artifacts-viewer.md): Create, preview, and refine documents, presentations, spreadsheets, and PDF files in ChatGPT

## Auth
- [Authentication](https://developers.openai.com/codex/auth.md): Sign-in methods for ChatGPT web and Codex clients

## Automations
- [Scheduled tasks](https://developers.openai.com/codex/automations.md): Schedule recurring tasks in ChatGPT

## Browser
- [Browser](https://developers.openai.com/codex/browser.md): Let ChatGPT research and interact with websites

## Build App
- [Build an app](https://developers.openai.com/codex/build-app.md): Build the MCP-backed app portion of a plugin, with optional UI from the Apps SDK

## Build Plugins
- [Build plugins](https://developers.openai.com/codex/build-plugins.md): Create, test, and distribute plugins for ChatGPT

## Build Skills
- [Build skills](https://developers.openai.com/codex/build-skills.md): Give Codex new capabilities and expertise

## Chrome Extension
- [Chrome extension](https://developers.openai.com/codex/chrome-extension.md): Use Chrome with ChatGPT while managing browser permissions, website approvals, and browsing data

## Cli
- [Codex CLI](https://developers.openai.com/codex/cli.md): Use Codex from your terminal and scripts.
- [Command line options](https://developers.openai.com/codex/cli/reference.md): Options and flags for the Codex terminal client
- [Slash commands in Codex CLI](https://developers.openai.com/codex/cli/slash-commands.md): Control Codex during interactive sessions

## Cli Customization
- [CLI customization](https://developers.openai.com/codex/cli-customization.md): Customize the Codex terminal interface, editor, completions, and shortcuts

## Cloud
- [Codex cloud](https://developers.openai.com/codex/cloud.md): Delegate work to Codex in isolated cloud environments.
- [Agent internet access](https://developers.openai.com/codex/cloud/internet-access.md): Control internet access for Codex cloud chats

## Code Review
- [Code review](https://developers.openai.com/codex/code-review.md): Review code changes across ChatGPT and Codex clients

## Codex Sdk
- [Codex SDK](https://developers.openai.com/codex/codex-sdk.md): Programmatically control local Codex agents

## Community
- [Codex for Open Source](https://developers.openai.com/codex/community/codex-for-oss.md): Open-source maintainers can apply for API credits, six months of ChatGPT Pro with Codex, and Codex Security.

## Computer Use
- [Computer Use](https://developers.openai.com/codex/computer-use.md): Let ChatGPT use desktop apps while it works

## Config File
- [Advanced Configuration](https://developers.openai.com/codex/config-file/config-advanced.md): More advanced configuration options for Codex local clients
- [Config basics](https://developers.openai.com/codex/config-file/config-basic.md): Learn the basics of configuring your local Codex client
- [Configuration Reference](https://developers.openai.com/codex/config-file/config-reference.md): Complete reference for Codex config.toml and requirements.toml
- [Environment variables](https://developers.openai.com/codex/config-file/environment-variables.md): Environment variables supported by Codex
- [Sample Configuration](https://developers.openai.com/codex/config-file/config-sample.md): A complete example config.toml you can copy and adapt

## Configuration
- [Configuration](https://developers.openai.com/codex/configuration.md): Configure ChatGPT and Codex developer tools, reusable workflows, and external tools

## Custom Prompts
- [Custom Prompts](https://developers.openai.com/codex/custom-prompts.md): Deprecated. Use skills for reusable prompts

## Customization
- [Chronicle](https://developers.openai.com/codex/customization/chronicle.md): Build Codex memories from recent screen context.
- [Customization](https://developers.openai.com/codex/customization/overview.md): How to customize Codex with project guidance, skills, MCP, and subagents
- [Memories](https://developers.openai.com/codex/customization/memories.md): How ChatGPT and Codex carry useful context forward across chats

## Cyber Safety
- [Cyber Safety](https://developers.openai.com/codex/cyber-safety.md): Cybersecurity safeguards and trusted access for Codex users

## Developers
- [Developers](https://developers.openai.com/codex/developers.md): Configure development workflows, environments, extensions, and integrations

## Enterprise
- [Access tokens](https://developers.openai.com/codex/enterprise/access-tokens.md): Create and manage access tokens for Codex programmatic workflows
- [Admin rollout guide](https://developers.openai.com/codex/enterprise/admin-setup.md): Plan, configure, and verify a ChatGPT Enterprise rollout across workspace and developer surfaces
- [Analytics API](https://developers.openai.com/codex/enterprise/analytics-api.md): Understand the purpose and administration boundary of the Codex Analytics API
- [ChatGPT usage limits and spend controls](https://developers.openai.com/codex/enterprise/usage-limits.md): Understand when ChatGPT workspace credit controls can affect plan-dependent Codex usage
- [Compliance API and audit events](https://developers.openai.com/codex/enterprise/compliance-api.md): Understand the purpose and administration boundary of the Compliance API
- [Deploy the Windows app](https://developers.openai.com/codex/enterprise/windows-deployment.md): Choose an enterprise installation and update path for the ChatGPT desktop app on Windows
- [Governance](https://developers.openai.com/codex/enterprise/governance.md): Choose the appropriate analytics, usage, and audit surface for each administration question
- [Groups and provisioning](https://developers.openai.com/codex/enterprise/groups-and-provisioning.md): Understand group membership sources and their workspace access boundary
- [Managed configuration](https://developers.openai.com/codex/enterprise/managed-configuration.md): Enforce runtime requirements across supported local clients and distribute managed defaults
- [Plugin controls](https://developers.openai.com/codex/enterprise/apps-and-connectors.md): Understand plugin availability, connector-backed capabilities, actions, and connected-system access
- [Roles and workspace permissions](https://developers.openai.com/codex/enterprise/roles-and-workspace-permissions.md): Separate ChatGPT workspace access from local runtime, API, plugin, and source-system controls
- [Skill controls](https://developers.openai.com/codex/enterprise/skills.md): Compare ChatGPT workspace, local filesystem, and plugin skill controls
- [Work mode admin FAQ](https://developers.openai.com/codex/enterprise/work-admin-faq.md): Manage access, data, governance, observability, usage, and incident controls for Work mode
- [Workspace analytics](https://developers.openai.com/codex/enterprise/workspace-analytics.md): Compare ChatGPT workspace analytics, Codex analytics, reporting APIs, and audit records
- [Workspace model availability](https://developers.openai.com/codex/enterprise/workspace-model-availability.md): Separate model access across ChatGPT, Codex in the ChatGPT desktop app, Codex CLI, the IDE extension, Codex cloud, and the OpenAI API Platform

## Environments
- [Cloud environments](https://developers.openai.com/codex/environments/cloud-environment.md): Customize dependencies and tools for Codex
- [Codex environments](https://developers.openai.com/codex/environments/modes.md): Choose where a Codex chat runs and how its files stay isolated
- [Local environments](https://developers.openai.com/codex/environments/local-environment.md): Configure common actions and setup scripts for worktrees
- [Worktrees](https://developers.openai.com/codex/environments/git-worktrees.md): Use Git worktrees in Codex in the ChatGPT desktop app to run chats in parallel

## Extend
- [Model Context Protocol](https://developers.openai.com/codex/extend/mcp.md): Give Codex access to third-party tools and context
- [Record & Replay](https://developers.openai.com/codex/extend/record-and-replay.md): Show Codex a workflow once and turn it into a reusable skill

## Feature Maturity
- [Feature Maturity](https://developers.openai.com/codex/feature-maturity.md): How to interpret feature maturity levels in ChatGPT releases

## Features
- [Features](https://developers.openai.com/codex/features.md): Explore workflows, capabilities, commands, and settings in ChatGPT
- [Codex Micro](https://developers.openai.com/codex/features/codex-micro.md): Your command center for Codex chats.

## Get Started With Work
- [Get started with Work mode](https://developers.openai.com/codex/get-started-with-work.md): Delegate substantial tasks to ChatGPT in Work mode and get reviewable results.

## Github Action
- [Codex GitHub Action](https://developers.openai.com/codex/github-action.md): Trigger Codex actions from GitHub Events

## Glossary
- [Glossary](https://developers.openai.com/codex/glossary.md): Definitions for common Codex terms and concepts

## Guides
- [Building an AI-Native Engineering Team](https://developers.openai.com/codex/guides/build-ai-native-engineering-team.md): How coding agents speed up the software development lifecycle

## Hooks
- [Hooks](https://developers.openai.com/codex/hooks.md): Run deterministic scripts during the Codex lifecycle

## Ide
- [Codex IDE extension](https://developers.openai.com/codex/ide.md): Use Codex beside your code and editor context.
- [Codex IDE extension commands](https://developers.openai.com/codex/ide/commands.md): Reference for Codex IDE extension commands and keyboard shortcuts
- [Codex IDE extension settings](https://developers.openai.com/codex/ide/settings.md): Reference for Codex IDE extension settings
- [Codex IDE extension slash commands](https://developers.openai.com/codex/ide/slash-commands.md): Reference for slash commands in the Codex IDE extension

## Image Generation
- [Image generation](https://developers.openai.com/codex/image-generation.md): Generate and edit images in ChatGPT

## Image Inputs
- [Image inputs](https://developers.openai.com/codex/image-inputs.md): Attach screenshots, diagrams, and visual references to ChatGPT prompts

## Import
- [Import from another agent](https://developers.openai.com/codex/import.md): Bring supported setup and recent work from another agent into ChatGPT.

## Integrated Terminal
- [Integrated terminal](https://developers.openai.com/codex/integrated-terminal.md): Run commands and inspect output without leaving your chat

## Learn
- [Best practices](https://developers.openai.com/codex/learn/best-practices.md): Getting started with Codex and proven practices for better results

## Long Running Work
- [Long-running work](https://developers.openai.com/codex/long-running-work.md): Keep multi-step work focused with clear outcomes and completion criteria

## Mcp Server
- [Use Codex with the Agents SDK](https://developers.openai.com/codex/mcp-server.md): Invoke Codex as an MCP server to build multi-agent development workflows

## Models
- [Models](https://developers.openai.com/codex/models.md): Meet the AI models that power Work mode and Codex

## Non Interactive Mode
- [Non-interactive mode](https://developers.openai.com/codex/non-interactive-mode.md): Use `codex exec` to run Codex in scripts and CI

## Notifications
- [Notifications](https://developers.openai.com/codex/notifications.md): Choose how you are notified when work needs attention

## Open Source
- [Open Source](https://developers.openai.com/codex/open-source.md): Open-source components of Codex and where to collaborate

## Overview
- [ChatGPT](https://developers.openai.com/codex/overview.md): Use ChatGPT for ambitious work and software development

## Permission Modes
- [Permissions](https://developers.openai.com/codex/permission-modes.md): Choose what ChatGPT can do on your computer and when it asks for approval

## Permissions
- [Permissions](https://developers.openai.com/codex/permissions.md): Configure beta Codex permission profiles for filesystem and network access

## Personalize
- [Personalize ChatGPT](https://developers.openai.com/codex/personalize.md): Set preferences and carry useful context across chats

## Pets
- [Pets](https://developers.openai.com/codex/pets.md): Choose an animated companion and follow chat activity across supported interfaces

## Plugins
- [Plugins](https://developers.openai.com/codex/plugins.md): Browse, install, and use plugins on supported ChatGPT and Codex surfaces

## Pricing
- [Pricing](https://developers.openai.com/codex/pricing.md): Work mode and Codex are included in your ChatGPT Free, Go, Plus, Pro, Business, Edu, or Enterprise plan

## Projects
- [Projects and chats](https://developers.openai.com/codex/projects.md): Keep related chats, files, and sources together in ChatGPT

## Prompting
- [Prompting](https://developers.openai.com/codex/prompting.md): Write useful prompts for Chat mode, Work mode, and Codex

## Quickstart
- [Quickstart](https://developers.openai.com/codex/quickstart.md): Start using ChatGPT on the web or in the desktop app.

## Reference
- [Troubleshooting](https://developers.openai.com/codex/reference/troubleshooting.md): FAQ and fixes for common ChatGPT desktop app issues

## Remote Connections
- [Remote connections](https://developers.openai.com/codex/remote-connections.md): Use Remote to access work on a connected computer, or connect the ChatGPT desktop app to projects over SSH

## Resources
- [Resources](https://developers.openai.com/codex/resources.md): Find Codex videos, community programs, and OpenAI resources

## Sandboxing
- [Sandbox](https://developers.openai.com/codex/sandboxing.md): How sandboxing works across ChatGPT and Codex clients
- [Auto-review](https://developers.openai.com/codex/sandboxing/auto-review.md): How Codex routes sandbox-boundary approvals through a reviewer agent

## Security
- [Codex Security](https://developers.openai.com/codex/security.md): Find and remediate vulnerabilities with the Codex Security plugin or Codex Security cloud.
- [Codex Security cloud FAQ](https://developers.openai.com/codex/security/faq.md): Common questions about Codex Security cloud.
- [Codex Security cloud setup](https://developers.openai.com/codex/security/setup.md): Set up Codex Security cloud, wait for initial findings, and improve results with threat model edits
- [Codex Security plugin changelog](https://developers.openai.com/codex/security/plugin/changelog.md): Notable user-facing changes to the Codex Security plugin.
- [Codex Security plugin quickstart](https://developers.openai.com/codex/security/plugin.md): Install the Codex Security plugin, run your first read-only scan, and review the result in Codex.
- [Export and track security findings](https://developers.openai.com/codex/security/plugin/export-findings.md): Create portable findings artifacts or prepare approval-gated issues and draft advisories from a completed scan.
- [Fix and verify security findings](https://developers.openai.com/codex/security/plugin/fix-findings.md): Turn accepted findings into minimal patches with focused regression evidence.
- [Improving the threat model](https://developers.openai.com/codex/security/threat-model.md): Refine the Codex Security context that ranks findings and helps you review faster
- [Propose security hardening](https://developers.openai.com/codex/security/plugin/security-hardening.md): Develop evidence-backed structural security improvements, compare tradeoffs, and prepare an implementation handoff.
- [Review code changes for security](https://developers.openai.com/codex/security/plugin/code-changes.md): Review pull requests and local changes for security regressions manually or in CI/CD.
- [Run a Codex Security scan](https://developers.openai.com/codex/security/plugin/scans.md): Scan an authorized repository or one scoped folder and review findings, coverage, and artifacts.
- [Run a deep security scan](https://developers.openai.com/codex/security/plugin/deep-scans.md): Run a slower, more thorough review of a repository or scoped folder.
- [Triage a backlog](https://developers.openai.com/codex/security/plugin/triage-backlog.md): Review existing security findings against a repository and order follow-up work.
- [Write vulnerability reports](https://developers.openai.com/codex/security/plugin/vulnerability-reports.md): Turn findings, disclosure notes, source, and proof-of-concept material into polished, source-backed vulnerability reports.

## Security Administration
- [Security](https://developers.openai.com/codex/security-administration.md): Manage permissions, security, and safety controls for ChatGPT and Codex developer tools

## Sites
- [Sites](https://developers.openai.com/codex/sites.md): Build and share hosted sites in ChatGPT

## Skills And Plugins
- [Skills & Plugins](https://developers.openai.com/codex/skills-and-plugins.md): Add reusable workflows, expertise, and connected tools to ChatGPT

## Submit Plugins
- [Submit plugins](https://developers.openai.com/codex/submit-plugins.md): Submit a plugin for review through the OpenAI Platform

## Third Party
- [Codex code review in GitHub](https://developers.openai.com/codex/third-party/github.md): Set up Codex code review for GitHub pull requests, request reviews with @codex review, enable automatic reviews, and customize review guidelines.
- [Use Codex in Linear](https://developers.openai.com/codex/third-party/linear.md): Run Codex chats from Linear issues
- [Use Codex in Slack](https://developers.openai.com/codex/third-party/slack.md): Ask Codex to work from Slack channels and threads

## Use Chatgpt
- [Use ChatGPT](https://developers.openai.com/codex/use-chatgpt.md): Learn how to ask for what you need, add useful context, and refine the result.

## Videos
- [Videos](https://developers.openai.com/codex/videos.md): Learn how to use Codex with demos, walkthroughs, and talks

## Visualizations
- [Visualizations](https://developers.openai.com/codex/visualizations.md): Turn ideas and information into interactive visual explanations in ChatGPT

## Web
- [ChatGPT on the web](https://developers.openai.com/codex/web.md): Use ChatGPT on the web to research, analyze, and create files.

## Web Search
- [Web search](https://developers.openai.com/codex/web-search.md): Search the web while you work in ChatGPT

## Whats New
- [What's new](https://developers.openai.com/codex/whats-new.md): A weekly digest of ChatGPT and Codex features that can change how you work

## Windows
- [ChatGPT desktop app for Windows](https://developers.openai.com/codex/windows/windows-app.md): Use the ChatGPT desktop app on Windows with native sandbox and PowerShell support
- [Windows sandbox](https://developers.openai.com/codex/windows/windows-sandbox.md): Configure and troubleshoot the native Codex sandbox on Windows
- [WSL](https://developers.openai.com/codex/windows/wsl.md): Run and troubleshoot Codex in Windows Subsystem for Linux
